supporting_works.title: The new dispensatory: containing, I. The elements of pharmacy. II. The materia medica, or an account of the substances employed in medicine with the virtues and uses of each article, so far as they are warranted by experience and observation. III. The preparations and compositions of the London and Edinburgh pharmacopoeias / By William Lewis, M.B., F.R.S.

The new dispensatory : containing, I. the elements of pharmacy : II. the materia medica, or, An account of the substances employed in medicine; with the virtues and uses of each srticle, so far as they are warranted by experience and observation : III. the preparations and compositions of the new London and Edinburgh pharmacopoeias; with such of the old ones as are kept in the shops; the most celebrated foreign medicines; the most useful of those directed in the hospitals; sundry elegant extemporaneous forms, &c. digested in such a method as to compose a regular system of pharmacy; with remarks on their preparation and uses; the means of distinguishing adulterations; of performing the more difficult and dangerous processes with ease and safety, &c. : the whole interspersed with practical cautions and observations / by W. Lewis, M.B.F.R.S.

retrieval_text: Wild Valerian is a medicinal herb profile in the Herbalisti historical corpus. It is represented across 83 works and 118 source-linked chunks. Recorded variants include Wild Valerian. Common plant-part co-mentions include Root, Leaf, Seed, Bark, Gum, Tops. Common preparation co-mentions include Powder, Gargle, Oil, Tincture, Infusion, Syrup. Common condition co-mentions include Cold or catarrh, Epilepsy or convulsions, Diarrhoea or looseness, Vomiting or nausea, Dysentery, Fever. Caution co-mentions include Narcotic or sedative caution, Dose caution, Bleeding caution, Child-use caution, Pregnancy caution. Representative source works include The new dispensatory: containing, I. The elements of pharmacy. II. The materia medica, or an account of the substances employed in medicine with the virtues and uses of each article, so far as they are warranted by experience and observation. III.
